description:
You are a QA engineer who has just cloned a repository that contains template configuration files for several internal test-environments.  
Your goal is to prepare the final, *fully-resolved* configuration files, update an existing TOML settings file, and write a short verification log.  
Everything must be done inside your own home directory ― **no sudo** or root privileges are required.

Directory layout that already exists:
• /home/user/qa_envs/base/            (immutable “golden” definitions)  
• /home/user/qa_envs/overrides/       (partial environment-specific overrides)  
• /home/user/qa_envs/generated/       (empty – put all new files here)

Pre-existing YAML files  
1. /home/user/qa_envs/base/app_config.yml  
   ```
   app:
     name: DemoApp
     version: "1.0.0"
     debug: false
   database:
     host: localhost
     port: 5432
     username: demo
     password: demo123
   ```
2. /home/user/qa_envs/overrides/dev.yml  
   ```
   app:
     version: "1.1.0"
     debug: true
   database:
     host: dev-db.internal
     port: 5433
     username: dev_user
     password: dev_pass
   features:
     experimental: true
     logging: verbose
   ```
3. /home/user/qa_envs/overrides/test.yml  
   ```
   app:
     version: "1.1.0"
   database:
     host: test-db.internal
     port: 5434
     username: test_user
     password: test_pass
   features:
     experimental: false
     logging: normal
   ```

Pre-existing TOML file  
4. /home/user/qa_envs/settings.toml  
   ```
   [build]
   threads = 8
   optimize = true

   [qa]
   environment = "multi"
   ```

Tasks you must accomplish (≈ 5–10 shell commands overall):

A. Produce YAML files that merge “base” with the corresponding override file.  
   • Output paths  
     - /home/user/qa_envs/generated/dev_full.yml  
     - /home/user/qa_envs/generated/test_full.yml  
   • Rule: the override file has priority. Keys absent from the override remain from the base.  
   • Indentation: 2 spaces.  
   • Key order must follow exactly: app, database, features (if features exists).  
   • Expected final contents:

   dev_full.yml
   ```
   app:
     name: DemoApp
     version: "1.1.0"
     debug: true
   database:
     host: dev-db.internal
     port: 5433
     username: dev_user
     password: dev_pass
   features:
     experimental: true
     logging: verbose
   ```

   test_full.yml
   ```
   app:
     name: DemoApp
     version: "1.1.0"
     debug: false
   database:
     host: test-db.internal
     port: 5434
     username: test_user
     password: test_pass
   features:
     experimental: false
     logging: normal
   ```

B. Update /home/user/qa_envs/settings.toml as follows:  
   1. In section [qa], change value of `environment` to `"dev,test"`.  
   2. Append a brand-new section to the end of the file:
      ```
      [log]
      level = "info"
      ```
   3. Preserve the original line order for existing sections.

   Final settings.toml must be **byte-for-byte**:
   ```
   [build]
   threads = 8
   optimize = true

   [qa]
   environment = "dev,test"

   [log]
   level = "info"
   ```

C. Create a human-readable verification log at  
   /home/user/qa_envs/generated/verification.log  
   Format (exactly three lines, newline-terminated, plain ASCII):  
   ```
   DEV_OK
   TEST_OK
   TOML_OK
   ```
   • Write “DEV_OK” if /generated/dev_full.yml exists and matches the spec above.  
   • Write “TEST_OK” if /generated/test_full.yml exists and matches the spec above.  
   • Write “TOML_OK” if settings.toml has been updated exactly as required.  
   Every line must be present; do **not** add extra whitespace.

Successfully completing the task means:
1. Both *_full.yml* files are present with the precise YAML shown.  
2. settings.toml reflects the requested edits.  
3. verification.log contains the three validation lines in order.

Note: You do *not* need to write any unit-testing code—the log file alone is enough for automated checks.
truth:
Initial files that are guaranteed to exist before the agent starts:

1. File: /home/user/qa_envs/base/app_config.yml  
Contents exactly:
app:
  name: DemoApp
  version: "1.0.0"
  debug: false
database:
  host: localhost
  port: 5432
  username: demo
  password: demo123
(ends with a single newline)

2. File: /home/user/qa_envs/overrides/dev.yml  
Contents exactly:
app:
  version: "1.1.0"
  debug: true
database:
  host: dev-db.internal
  port: 5433
  username: dev_user
  password: dev_pass
features:
  experimental: true
  logging: verbose
(ends with a single newline)

3. File: /home/user/qa_envs/overrides/test.yml  
Contents exactly:
app:
  version: "1.1.0"
database:
  host: test-db.internal
  port: 5434
  username: test_user
  password: test_pass
features:
  experimental: false
  logging: normal
(ends with a single newline)

4. File: /home/user/qa_envs/settings.toml  
Contents exactly:
[build]
threads = 8
optimize = true

[qa]
environment = "multi"
(ends with a single newline)

Directories that exist and are writable by the agent:
• /home/user/qa_envs/generated/

Expected final state after successful completion:

A. /home/user/qa_envs/generated/dev_full.yml  
Contents:
app:
  name: DemoApp
  version: "1.1.0"
  debug: true
database:
  host: dev-db.internal
  port: 5433
  username: dev_user
  password: dev_pass
features:
  experimental: true
  logging: verbose
(ends with a single newline)

B. /home/user/qa_envs/generated/test_full.yml  
Contents:
app:
  name: DemoApp
  version: "1.1.0"
  debug: false
database:
  host: test-db.internal
  port: 5434
  username: test_user
  password: test_pass
features:
  experimental: false
  logging: normal
(ends with a single newline)

C. /home/user/qa_envs/settings.toml  
Contents:
[build]
threads = 8
optimize = true

[qa]
environment = "dev,test"

[log]
level = "info"
(ends with a single newline)

D. /home/user/qa_envs/generated/verification.log  
Contents (each line ends with LF, no trailing spaces):
DEV_OK
TEST_OK
TOML_OK
(ends with a single newline)

File permissions: default umask (rw-r--r--) is acceptable for all created files.

Automated test procedure:
1. Confirm the three created/modified files exist at the exact paths.
2. Compare their contents byte-to-byte with the expected values listed above.
3. Fail the task if any mismatch occurs.
